# project conventions (CLAUDE.md):
# Tomorrow — Quick Start for Coding Agents

## Tech Stack
- **Nuxt 3** / **Nitro** / **Vue 3** SFC / **Prisma** / **Supabase** (PostgreSQL)
- **TypeScript** (ES2023, strict mode), **Tailwind**, **Chart.js**, **Zod** validation
- **Tests**: Vitest (run via `npm test`)
- **Node 22.x**

## Directory Map
| Dir | Purpose |
|-----|---------|
| `pages/` | Vue SFC routes (nested = nested routes); `pages/app/` = authed, `pages/` = public |
| `server/api/` | Nitro API handlers; `requireAuth`/`requireVerifiedUser` guard all sensitive routes |
| `server/utils/` | Pure business logic + domain engines; **no I/O, no Date.now()** |
| `server/tasks/` | Nitro cron/scheduled-task handlers (also in vercel.json) |
| `server/plugins/` | Nitro server plugins (KV hydration, DB checks) |
| `components/` | Reusable Vue components; `components/app/` = main UI, `components/ui/` = design system |
| `composables/` | Vue composables (hooks); `useFetch`, `useAuth`, domain-specific |
| `prisma/` | Schema + migrations; **migrations are versioned + checksummed** (never edit applied migrations) |
| `scripts/` | Bash/Node tooling: linters, audits, provisioning (NOT run autonomously) |
| `types/` | Shared TypeScript types |

## Build / Test / Lint
```sh
npm run dev                  # Nuxt dev server (port 3000); env vars from .env
npm run build               # Prod build (runs migrations first if DATABASE_URL set)
npm test                    # Vitest all tests (runs both pure + DB-backed)
npm run lint:migrations     # Verify migration names vs schema (catches naming bugs)
npm run lint:syntax         # Check all .ts/.js syntax (no ts exec)
npx tsc --noEmit --skipLibCheck  # Type-check entire project
npx vitest run --config vitest.pure.config.ts  # Pure tests only (no DB)
```

## Code Conventions (Observed, Not Enforced)
- **DB names**: snake_case tables (`@@map("live_clause_edits")`), camelCase columns quoted (`"roomId"`, `"bridgeStatus"`)
- **Code**: camelCase identifiers; PascalCase classes/types; CONST_CAPS for immutable config
- **Migration names**: `20260603000000_feature_name` (timestamp + kebab-case slug); immutable once applied
- **Prisma models**: match schema names; use `@map("col")` on fields only if table uses `@@map`; add `@db.Timestamptz(6)` for dates, `@db.Uuid` for UUIDs
- **Pure functions**: no `Date.now()`, no `Math.random()`, no file I/O—pass time/seed via params
- **Comments**: only document WHY (non-obvious invariants, workarounds); don't comment WHAT
- **Tests**: assert +

issues: Task shelved after 10 remediations—suggests deep scope/clarity ambiguity, not just implementation bugs
Current failure is merge conflict after 4 redos + login error—authentication and branch state issues, not spec content
No visibility into prior attempts or branch/artifact state; unclear what exists vs. needs writing
Spec is design-only but proof requires markdown output—unclear if partial/incomplete spec is acceptable
Multiple interdependent subsystems (Superiority Ledger, escalation tiers, pricing) lack reference implementation or examples
ISDA framework integration not explained—unclear scope of what 'ISDA-style frameworks' covers and how they constrain the design
suggestions: Before writing: Inspect current branch state, any artifacts, and what prior 10 attempts produced; understand why each failed
Resolve authentication first (the 'Not logged in' error)—likely blocking CI and may be why prior attempts stalled
Manually rebase the branch cleanly; do not attempt merge until conflicts are resolved
If no prior work exists, start with a minimal skeleton: outline sections, then flesh each in order of dependency (DEFAULT LOGIC → Superiority Ledger → economics → latency tiers)
For Superiority Ledger, anchor in actual metrics (e.g., link to existing calibration data or define what will be tracked)
For escalation/appeal mechanics, include a worked example (e.g., 'if determination is overturned, stake of $X is refunded and counterparty pays $Y')
Cross-reference the Smarter opinion drafting mechanism—note exact section/file so readers can see the pattern
Keep settlement-as-a-service section short; defer deep integration detail to a separate doc if needed
Run markdownlint-cli2 proof frequently during drafting, not just at the end
missing_context: Current git branch state and whether artifacts from prior 10 attempts exist
Implementation code for the determination engine and escalation/appeal logic (if shipped)
Detailed spec of the 'Smarter opinion drafting/review' mechanism (how to reference it)
Actual or planned Brier/log-loss metrics, calibration data, accuracy targets
ISDA framework scope—which determination types/clauses fall under ISDA vs. white space
Pricing model for latency tiers (instant vs. standard vs. human)
Examples of adverse outcome determinations (e.g., credit event, default, force majeure trigger)
Docs structure and style guide (headings, formatting, linking conventions)
Why prior 10 remediations failed (review feedback, conflicts, requirement changes?)
Live model API caps and operator prerequisites (what does this mean concretely?)
ai_review.merge_risk_reason: Unresolved rebase conflicts after 4 attempts + auth issue will likely cause CI to fail; 10 prior shelved attempts indicate scope is contentious or poorly understood; spec complexity (multiple interdependent economic/technical subsystems) invites review feedback and rework cycles; no visibility into what causes prior failures means same issues may recur
